label: Simple 4-Prompt Experimental

prompt_stages.pass_1_creative_story: Using the SOURCE MATERIAL and TONE GUARDRAILS provided in the run context below, write one complete radio-drama story. Hard limits: at most 8 scenes, 2-4 speaking characters, 250 words per scene. Prose only - do not output JSON in this pass.
prompt_stages.pass_2_creative_ledger_fill: Convert the story from the previous pass into the production story ledger. Emit ONLY valid JSON matching the LEDGER SCHEMA provided in the run context - use exactly its field names; do not invent fields. Include cast, lines, beats, source metadata, coda/source note, and visual directives.
prompt_stages.pass_3_technical_schema_cleanup: Repair the JSON from the previous pass so it satisfies the LEDGER SCHEMA in the run context exactly: required fields present, enum values legal, ids consistent, no extra fields. Emit ONLY the repaired JSON.
prompt_stages.pass_4_technical_ledger_audit: Audit the JSON from the previous pass: cast vs line speakers, character ids, beat ids, line ids, continuity, source note, visual directives. Emit ONLY the corrected JSON, or the single line AUDIT_FAILED: <reason> if it cannot be made valid.

examples: A stronger model writes a rich story first, then fills and repairs the ledger in technical passes.
tone_guardrails: Python should validate and repair structure, not secretly co-write the story.
No fallback to legacy_many_pass if this experiment fails.
Adaptive cleanup may continue only under a hard max pass cap and deterministic validator failures.
source_requirements: Any selected source bank must provide a valid source packet and story pack.
ledger_validation_notes: Pass 4 must check cast vs line speakers, character ids, beat ids, line ids, source metadata, coda/source note, and visual directives.
Optional adaptive cleanup stops when deterministic validators pass or max_cleanup_passes is reached.
